frameworks.name: global movement suppression by STN
frameworks.one_line: STN broadly inhibits movement via downstream basal ganglia connections.

frameworks.name: beta oscillation movement inhibition
frameworks.one_line: STN beta-band LFP power gates motor suppression and firing.

frameworks.name: inhomogeneous population code
frameworks.one_line: Distinct STN sub-populations encode kinematics versus action switches.

core_question: How does human STN activity respond when an ongoing reaching action must be replaced with an alternative trajectory?
key_assumptions: STN single-unit and LFP activity can be recorded in humans during reaching
switching mid-action is a stronger test than withholding an uninitiated plan
manipulations: mid-action instructions to switch reaching trajectory
comparison of ongoing-movement versus action-switch epochs
key_findings: STN activity decreases during action switches rather than increasing
STN beta LFP power does not rise and does not entrain firing during switches
one STN sub-population encodes movement kinematics and direction
another STN sub-population encodes unexpected action switches
interpretations: STN does not act as a global movement suppressor in this switch context
STN supports an elaborate code for planning actions and changing plans
